e1.creativeStrategy: Signal-path metaphor: dotted neural line enters from left, travels through three recognition-anchor icons, culminates in soft glow behind CTA. Editorial Split layout. Signal-Path Travel Animation GIF with nodes lighting up sequentially. Custom SVG signal-path illustration with three glowing nodes representing three family stories.

e2.creativeStrategy: Testimonial wall with subtle neural pulse line splitting into three paths (one per family story) converging at CTA as unified signal-received glow. Single-column cascade with asymmetrical testimonial cards. Signal Progress Meter SVG with three branching neural nodes.

e3.creativeStrategy: Skepticism-acknowledging close email. Objection D3 (hope-fear cycle). Minimal hero, guarantee-focused layout. Section structure: Hook, Validation, Recognition Anchors, Mechanism, Guarantee, Three-Family Testimonial, CTA + Offer, Lena Sign-Off. 60-day guarantee as honest protocol not sales tactic.

e1.creativeStrategy: Permission to pause backed by guarantee. Speed of send (10 min) catches hesitation before rationalization. Minimal hero — permission-frame email. Clean, no product imagery, focused on reassurance. Lena 4-step formula (Observation, Validation, Mechanism, Permission).

e2.creativeStrategy: Deep barrier — hope-fear cycle. Full Whether/Or/If 5-dimension sentence for cross-level coverage. Minimal validation-focused email, text-heavy with warmth. Three-Family Testimonial Pattern in body. CTA intentionally soft.

e1.creativeStrategy: Softest touch in recovery funnel. Educational bridge connecting symptom to mechanism. Re-engage with symptom recognition (not product push), Validate the struggle, Educate on interoception mechanism, Introduce BSL concept without hard sell. ZERO urgency. Keep reading CTA — education-first positioning.

e2.creativeStrategy: Social proof pivot. Single job: make every abandoned browser feel seen, then show parents like them found the missing piece. Review count (16,511) as primary visual element. No product mention until CTA. GF-D lever included.

e3.creativeStrategy: Guarantee-driven close with permission-not-to-buy. Visual metaphor of a held-open door. Door illustration grows wider as reader scrolls. Signal-Dot Pulse CSS animation. Threshold Glow easter egg.

e1.creativeStrategy: Clean unboxing-like structure. Top half crisp and functional (order details, tracking), bottom half unfolds into soft family-warmth visuals. Z-Axis Cascade layout. Signal Arrival illustration (dotted neural pathway converging to emerald glow).

e2.creativeStrategy: Package journey — dotted trajectory line from warehouse to home icon. Warm + functional. NO CTA button per S2 spec — pure fulfillment. Package Journey Line SVG with signal-wave bumps. 3-Layer Exploded View illustration.

e3.creativeStrategy: Unboxing moment, warm anticipation. Package icon transforms into open box revealing underwear, signal-path dotted line flowing toward CTA. Tracking Signal Path Animation, dot traveling dotted line from gray to green. Signal Dots in three-step cards.

e4.creativeStrategy: Delivery moment — package arriving, being opened, revealing learning journey inside. Unfolding Signal Diagram SVG. Signal Path Pulse + 'Uh-Oh' Awareness Wave motion. Editorial product shot on warm cream linen.

e1.creativeStrategy: Care instruction card arriving at exactly the right moment — Day 3, when parent has just started washing routine. Single wash bag visual (SVG mesh bag with emerald accents). Utility email — NO animation. Instructional, trustworthy, not sales.

e2.creativeStrategy: Split-frame visual showing two-layer protection concept. Cutaway illustration of mattress protector layers in Ethereal Glass style. Signal-path dotted line arches from child through protector to mattress. Practical problem-solving, not emotional.

e1.creativeStrategy: Kitchen-table conversation. Four expandable symptom cards like handwritten sticky notes. Cards fade in sequentially. Four card accent stripes encode four avatar levels (never named).

e2.creativeStrategy: Parents don't want forms they want to feel seen. Level detection as recognition. No CTA button - the cards ARE the interaction. Dashed Not sure variant.

d0.creativeStrategy: Week 1 reset. 8-part cross-level architecture. Mint badge. Easter egg: Hidden Calibration Table in testimonials.

d7.creativeStrategy: Week 1 plateau validation. Radio analogy (static before signal). Pure education no purchase CTA.

d14.creativeStrategy: Two-week milestone. 7 recognition anchors. Graduated success. Reply CTA.

d21.creativeStrategy: Three-week checkpoint. 6 Recognition Anchors. Bridges to Flows 12 and 13.

e1.creativeStrategy: Day 28 bundle upgrade. Hook with symptom recognition. Consistency beats intensity - more pairs = no wash gaps = uninterrupted signal. Three-Family Testimonial Pattern. L3 guard: 1pr to 3+3 max NEVER 5+5.

e2.creativeStrategy: Day 35 rotation reinforcement. Three parents added more pairs (social proof normalization). Rotation = more signal exposure = faster neural wiring. FAQ section addressing top 3 objections inline.

e3.creativeStrategy: Day 60 final extended upsell. Highest-risk period for abandonment. Validates hybrid progress state. 60-day science checkpoint. Product moment: next-phase tools (Targets Pack, Reward Chart, E-book).

d30.creativeStrategy: Day 30 not a deadline a benchmark. Signal-Strength Indicator contraption (3-stage journey). 5 Recognition Anchors. The absence of visible progress is not the absence of progress.

d45.creativeStrategy: Day 45 the quiet before the signal. Most critical period invisible. Validates plateau normalizes silence. Nicholson 2019 science + 30-60 second feedback explanation. Permission-framed upsell.

d60.creativeStrategy: 60-day checkpoint that meets every child where they are. Three-path validation all outcomes equal. Closing: You did. (single-word acknowledgment). 7 hidden easter eggs including Trust your read signature.

e1.creativeStrategy: Founder story from Mary S. NOT Lena never mixed. Mary sits on bathroom floor, research discovery of interoception, signal vs assumption insight. No purchase CTA trust-building only. 60-day guarantee in P.S. Mission statement with triple-Whether permission frame.

e1.creativeStrategy: Lowest-intent recovery — educational first impression, not a sales email. Hook with universal symptom recognition via 'Whether...Or...If...' pattern. SPARK 49% stat as normalization. Interoception bridge mechanism explanation. Three-Family testimonial pattern (verbal, sensory, non-verbal). Soft CTA 'Keep exploring'. Zero product mention.

Universal objection: 'I've tried everything.' Visual metaphor: jigsaw puzzle with missing piece. Names failed methods explicitly (sticker charts, timed sits, BCBA protocols). Reframes as signal-gap problem, not parenting failure. SPARK 49% stat. Soft CTA to explore.

e2.creativeStrategy: Address 'Does it work?' objection. Lead with science evidence — Nicholson 2019, Barmpagiannis 2025 systematic review of 49 studies. Mechanism deep-dive on interoception. Three family testimonials showing different timelines. CTA: explore mechanism.

e3.creativeStrategy: Address price objection. Cost-per-use comparison (pull-ups vs BSL underwear). 60-day guarantee as risk reversal. Show long-term savings. Testimonial from parent who calculated the math.

e4.creativeStrategy: Address fear of failure. Honest timelines (2-8 weeks for first signal). Normalize non-linear progress. 60-day guarantee as primary trust lever. Testimonial from parent who saw no change for 3 weeks then breakthrough.

e5.creativeStrategy: Validate 'not for us' decision. No pressure to convert. Permission-not-to-buy (V12). Offer alternative resources. Plant seed for future return. Testimonial from parent who came back 3 months later.

e6.creativeStrategy: Final FAQ objection handler. Summary of all objections addressed. Unique differentiator: body-signal learning vs every other method. Final permission-based CTA. Lena personal sign-off with longer story.

e1.creativeStrategy: Lowest-pressure re-engagement. Text-only, no hero image. 'The Pause Is Part of the Process.' Validates disengagement as natural. SPARK 49% stat as normalization. Reply-based CTA (not a button). Zero product push.

e2.creativeStrategy: Troubleshooting guide format. Three diagnostic cards: (1) Won't wear — 30-min intervals, (2) No awareness — increase wear to 3+ hours, (3) Regression — hold steady. Verbatim parent notes. Line-art icons for each roadblock. Dual CTA: button + reply link.

components.hero: None — content IS the hero (troubleshooting cards)

e3.creativeStrategy: Final check-in before frequency reduces. Single concrete action: 3 consecutive hours, 3 days in a row. Custom illustration: 'Soft Reset Still Life' (chair + underwear + sunlight). Permission-framed CTA. Warm rose accent (#D4A89B).

e4.creativeStrategy: Soft landing — final touch, not ultimatum. 'The door is always open.' Custom illustration: door ajar with warm glow, paper airplane slipped through. 60-day guarantee restarts whenever ready. Permission-not-to-buy (V12). Cream + brand green palette.

e1-gf.creativeStrategy: Universal symptom hook. Interoception pathway explanation. 63-child study (59% couldn't tell when they needed to urinate). Body-signal learning introduction. Permission CTA. Lena's personal story (sons trained at 6 and 8).
